name: Directory
comment:
An absolutely located directory containing a list of items.

@docs Directory, back, decoder, items, path

unions.name: Directory
unions.comment: An absolutely located directory containing a list of items.

values.name: back
values.comment: Get the parent directory of a `Directory`

values.name: decoder
items
values.comment: Get a `List` of `Item`s in a `Directory`.

values.name: path
values.comment: Get the path to a `Directory`.

name: Directory.File
comment:
A `File` located relative to its parent directory.

@docs File, decoder, extension, stem

aliases.name: File
aliases.comment: A `File` located relative to its parent directory.

values.name: decoder
extension
values.comment: Get the file type of a file, if it has one.

values.name: stem
values.comment: Get the part of the file name before its type.

name: Directory.Item

unions.name: Item
unions.comment:
An `Item` within a `Directory` directory; either a `File` or a `SubDirectory`.
`Item`s are located relative to their parent directory.

values.name: decoder

name: Directory.Selector

unions.name: Selector
unions.comment:
A `Directory` with a selected `Item`.
You can convert the selected `Item` to an absolute `Path`.

values.name: back
values.comment: Get the path to `selector`'s parent directory.

values.name: forward
values.comment: Get the `Path` to `selector`s focused `Item`. You could get a `File` or a `SubDirectory`!

values.name: list
values.comment: Get a `SelectList` of `Item`s in `Selector`.

values.name: path
values.comment: Get the path to `selector`s directory.

values.name: reselect
values.comment: Move `selector`'s focus to the `Item` at `index`.

values.name: selected
values.comment: Get the selected `Item` in `selector`.

values.name: withItem
values.comment: Create a `Selector` from `directory`, with focus on the `Item` at `index`.

name: Directory.SubDirectory
comment:
A `Directory` located relative to its parent directory.

@docs SubDirectory, decoder, name

aliases.name: SubDirectory
aliases.comment: A `Directory` located relative to its parent directory. Unlike a `Directory`, a `SubDirectory` has no knowledge of its contents.

values.name: decoder
name
values.comment: Get the name of a `SubDirectory`.

name: Path
comment:
An absolute path to either a file or a directory. Give these to your backend to load them into Elm!

@docs Path, decoder, encode

unions.name: Path
unions.comment: An absolute path to either a file or a directory. Give these to your backend to load them into Elm!

values.name: decoder
encode

name: Path.Directory
comment:
An absolute directory path. Give this to your backend to load new directories into Elm!

@docs Directory, decoder, encode, root

aliases.name: Directory
aliases.comment: An absolute directory path. Give this to your backend to load new directories into Elm!

values.name: decoder
encode
root
values.comment: The root directory. You'll probably load your first data from here.

name: Path.File
comment:
An absolute file path.

@docs File, decoder, directory, encode, extension, stem

values.name: decoder
directory
values.comment: Get the directory that contains the file.

values.name: encode
extension
values.comment: Get the type of a file, if it has one.

values.name: stem
values.comment: Get the part of a file name before its type.
